Using Resources From Multiple Threads

Direct3D is not thread-safe; in particular, there is no synchronization to prevent multiple commands from being written into the push-buffer at the same time. The title must guarantee that only one thread will ever be calling a function that pushes commands into the push-buffer at any given time.

A title should never try to use Direct3D from multiple threads if any Direct3D object has private data set on it. All private data is stored on one list that will be easily corrupted if manipulated from multiple threads.

Direct3D does have several functions that do not manipulate any global state and therefore can be called from any thread, although the performance counters for these functions may become corrupted. These include:

  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateTexture
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateVolumeTexture
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateCubeTexture
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateVertexBuffer
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateIndexBuffer
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreatePalette
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateRenderTarget
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateDepthStencilSurface
  • IDirect3DDevice8::CreateImageSurface
  • IDirect3DDevice8::IsBusy
  • IDirect3DDevice8::IsFencePending
  • IDirect3DResource8::IsBusy
  • IDirect3DResource8::Register
  • IDirect3DTexture8::GetLevelDesc
  • IDirect3DTexture8::UnlockRect
  • IDirect3DVolumeTexture8::GetLevelDesc
  • IDirect3DVolumeTexture8::UnlockBox
  • IDirect3DCubeTexture8::GetLevelDesc
  • IDirect3DCubeTexture8::UnlockRect
  • IDirect3DVertexBuffer8::GetDesc
  • IDirect3DVertexBuffer8::Unlock
  • IDirect3DIndexBuffer8::GetDesc
  • IDirect3DIndexBuffer8::Unlock
  • IDirect3DPalette8::GetSize
  • IDirect3DPalette8::Unlock
  • IDirect3DSurface8::GetDesc
  • IDirect3DSurface8::UnlockRect
  • IDirect3DVolume8::GetDesc
  • IDirect3DVolume8::UnlockBox
  • XGSetCubeTextureHeader
  • XGSetSurfaceHeader
  • XGSetTextureHeader
  • XGSetVolumeTextureHeader

The following methods can be called on a resource as long as the Lock field of the resource is set to zero and the resource is not set on IDirect3DDevice8. The Lock field is automatically set to zero if IDirect3DResource8::IsBusy is called and returns FALSE.

  • IDirect3DTexture8::LockRect
  • IDirect3DVolumeTexture8::LockBox
  • IDirect3DCuveTexture8::LockRect
  • IDirect3DPalette8::Lock
  • IDirect3DSurface8::LockRect
  • IDirect3DVolume8::LockBox

IDirect3DResource8::Release can be called with the same restrictions as the Lock methods as long as it will release the very last reference to the resource to cause it to be destroyed. The reference counting code is not thread-safe and may corrupt the resource if it is called from multiple threads at the same time.

IDirect3DVertexBuffer8::Lock can be called with the same restrictions as the other resource's Lock method as long as D3DLOCK_NOFLUSH is passed to inhibit the lock from flushing the vertex cache.

IDirect3DIndexBuffer8 and IDirect3DPushBuffer8 can both be used without setting them on the device. The title has to guarantee that neither IDirect3DDevice8::DrawVertices nor IDirect3DDevice8::RunPushBuffer are being called on another thread with this resource when either locking or destroying the resource.

Both IDirect3DDevice8::InsertCallback and IDirect3DDevice8::SetVerticalBlankCallback take method pointers that will be called at DPC time. These methods will not be synchronized with the Direct3D and should be treated as if they are being called on a separate thread. Also, much of the kernel and XTL functionality cannot be used at DPC time and these methods should be as short as possible. You should assume that you can only manipulate the state in your own code or set an event which will allow a background thread to do work.
